item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm fixated on that title; how does "a canary" relate to prime number theory and spheres of annihilation?
The only reference that comes to mind is "canary in a coal mine", an early warning system to detect danger before the problem gets serious.

Then I think of -B. Someone/thing is peppering this number around in such a way that it is likely to trip up folks working on the theorem.
So if this 21 digit integer is "the canary", what is it serving as a warning about that is worse than utter disintegration globes?

There is an IRL metaphysics theory that the world we know is really some sort of simulation, think The Matrix. The only logical way to prove that idea would be to find the limits of the program. The clipping plane where the reality program stops rendering properly, if you will.

Maybe the calculations to verify this conjecture will inevitably stumble on one of those edges. Maybe in the 22-25 digit range the "math" subroutine of reality just stops working.
Our "someone" spreading -B could be the IT guy in charge of making sure the reality servers don't crash; and 1446 is his hacked-together automatic system stability tool.
It's set to target elements of the reality program that would crash the whole system if allowed to keep running, then "deletes" them.

It's not a clean solution, causing instability in all sorts of nearby processes like "chemistry" and "human beings". But it was only meant as a short-term hotfix! Then the dev team got laid off, there hasn't been a patch for the core system since initial launch 14 billion years ago, there are about 5000 user-reported glitches on the mainlist of known bugs…

Popular opinion is that the studio is just milking the player base for all it's worth until the release of Reality 2 next fiscal epoch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this entertained me, but more importantly, it made me think. there's… something here. some kind of overarching story or concept that either i'm not educated enough to understand or deliberately isn't being revealed. i could be wrong, but i smell a mystery, and one that's compellingly told. mysteries are always a good time, even if i never get the answer. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given recent progress in terms of distributed computing, you might want to increase the number of digits in the anomalous string.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just used it to signify "28890 seconds before the experiment officially began". Is there some alternative notation I should be using?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-28890s

Wait, don't computers store time as unsigned integers? Other than that, I overall liked how weird this whole thing was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Science-y suggestion:

The vacuum briefly created by SCP-1466-A will typically suck in and subsequently disperse these chemicals into the surrounding area.

"The voids left by annihilated matter will typically implode, with the subsequent shock wave dispersing the generated chemicals into the surrounding area."

(See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cavitation)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially upvoted, but then changed it to a non-vote. I dug the first half as the disparate concepts of the anomalous integer and the spheres was intriguing and I was very curious to see what happened next. Turns out, uh, nothing much does, and it just (in my mind), all kind of fizzles out at the end. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of "Keter number", but the actual effects aren't terribly interesting and the crosstest doesn't add anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really, really not clear on the link between disproving the conjecture and the spheres of doom. If there's some scientific relation between this theorem being wrong and matter decaying, it's not at all communicated.

As far as I can tell, this just seems like some extradimensional beings having pranked us by setting a jape with a deliberate condition, and then nudging us when we aren't falling for the trap fast enough. The theorem seems completely incidental.

-1, I guess. The nudging and interference is somewhat interesting, but I really don't see the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My assumption was that it's a play on "canary in a coal mine" and that this is a leading indicator of something catastrophic in the offing, but I could be wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm totally okay with the concept of something violating previously accepted laws of mathematics, since everything else here is doing the same to other precepts of consensus reality. So I want to like this.

As written, though, it's just not really interesting to me. The effects of the anomaly seem sort of random and kind of low-stakes, especially since this is being intentionally propagated on some level. I suspect there's something I'm not getting here that will make this more coherent, but I'm just not seeing it at the moment.

I was hoping the cross link would help this to make more sense, but it seems to be there more for wikiwalk purposes than explanation. I'm not against that as such, but I was hoping for a little more.

It's not bad, but it's not doing much for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any hints for how the article's title relates to its contents, for one of the denser people like me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enjoyed this. I understand just enough mathematics to get the idea (I think), which helps. It makes me think of the space-time continuum having an allergic reaction.
+1

item.title: hey what's up gamers welcome to my channel if you like what you see be sure to like comment and subscribe
item.content:encoded:
hey so this is my new scp, fucking finally i'm done with finals

thanks to everyone who reviewed this in chat, especially gaffsey

i had the scp-1466 slot occupied with an scp already, but in retrospect i was unimpressed with its quality, so once i had something better to fill the slot i deleted it
